The Company

Two arms.
One compounding engine.

Most AI startups die at the customer-access stage. We sequenced WaveSix to neutralize that risk: advisory becomes our discovery and GTM engine; the product studio turns recurring patterns into recurring revenue.

— 01
Transformation Advisory
We diagnose where AI creates real enterprise value and accompany clients through implementation. Senior, AI-native, EU-domiciled.
— 02
AI Product Studio
We build and deploy AI products — bespoke for enterprise clients, and scalable IP born from patterns we see in advisory.
— 03
AI-Native Delivery
A meaningful share of internal and external delivery is already executed by AI agents. Structurally faster, leaner, more margin-resilient than legacy consulting.
The Phased Strategy

Phase 0 cleared.
Phase 1 in execution.

Most AI startups fail not on technology, but on customer access — they build products without a credible path to enterprise buyers. We sequenced WaveSix to neutralize this risk.

Phase 0 · Done

Brand & market access

Win and deliver for marquee enterprise clients. Build trusted relationships at decision-maker level. Earn the right to be the AI partner of choice. The stage where most AI startups stall — we have cleared it.

The Product Strategy

Recurring patterns,
productized.

Advisory mandates surface recurring enterprise needs. We systematize them. Two flagships are in active rollout — both with a built-in customer base waiting on the other side of the door.

— PRODUCT 01

AI Transformation Operating System

50 → 10,000+ employees 3–5× cheaper than incumbents 10 days to first assessment EU AI Act / GDPR / BetrVG
— Problem

Classical consulting is slow, shallow, and expensive. Leadership-only interviews. Generic roadmaps. Zero implementation accountability. Companies cannot see themselves with enough granularity to know where AI actually creates value.

— Solution

AI agents conduct parallel 30-minute interviews across the entire organization, building a granular operational map of workflows, tools, frictions, and bottlenecks. Output: a prioritized, feasibility-validated transformation roadmap, paired with continuous upskilling and adoption tracking — augmented by human experts and forward-deployed engineers for implementation.

— PRODUCT 02

Adaptive GenAI Academy

Per-seat recurring Langdock · Copilot · ChatGPT-E Synthetic trainer avatars Role-aware enablement
— Problem

Tool licenses are bought; adoption fails. Generic e-learning does not move the needle because it is decoupled from the user's actual role, platform stack, and company context.

— Solution

A self-individualizing GenAI academy that adapts to each user's role, platform, and proficiency level. Content delivered by synthetic trainer avatars — typically modeled on actual employees from the client organization — and tailored to the company's own use cases (e.g., Langdock-specific or Copilot-specific enablement).

Market & Positioning

An open seat
at the European table.

Europe needs an AI-native, EU-domiciled service provider that combines three things no incumbent satisfies. WaveSix occupies that gap — with proven enterprise clients, a senior operator team, and product IP already in market.

The Ask

A €5M round to
compound an unfair advantage.

Accelerate the productization of two emerging SaaS plays, scale our engineering bench, and consolidate WaveSix's position as Europe's leading AI-native services firm.

€5M
Tech & Engineering Hiring
Senior AI engineers, forward-deployed engineers, and product talent to scale Products 1 & 2 and broaden the roadmap.
Product R&D
Accelerate both flagship products to multi-tenant, self-serve maturity. Convert advisory revenue into recurring product revenue.
European GTM
Build out an enterprise sales motion beyond DACH. Attach the product layer to a wider European logo footprint.
Platform Investments
Strengthen our internal AI-native delivery infrastructure — the source of our structural margin advantage over legacy consulting.
— The 36-month ambition
Establish WaveSix Labs as the leading AI-native service provider in Europe — measured by enterprise logo penetration, productized ARR mix, and category-defining brand recognition.
